messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: If anyone is in the south Dayton area, I have both North and South aiming repeater nodes. They are both Heltec V3’s hooked up to log periodic antennas, both indoors. I’ve also added a room server too, with the default “hello” password. (edited)
messages.content_html: If anyone is in the south Dayton area, I have both North and South aiming repeater nodes. They are both Heltec V3’s hooked up to log periodic antennas, both indoors. I’ve also added a room server too, with the default “hello” password. (edited)

messages.author: Shaderu, the Fox Jester
messages.text: As long as the antennas have decent vertical separation it shouldn't interfere with each other that much

messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: The separation of the antennas might be a key part. The frequencies are close enough that they might interfere with each other. Worth experimenting with before full on committing. Btw heading out now so any replies will be a bit later.
messages.content_html: The separation of the antennas might be a key part. The frequencies are close enough that they might interfere with each other. Worth experimenting with before full on committing. Btw heading out now so any replies will be a bit later.

messages.author: Shaderu, the Fox Jester

messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: I’d ask around to see if anyone else locally is interested in playing with mesh comms. It seems like most of us are located in or near the bigger cities, and that’s where activity is starting. So unless you are setting up a network for your family and friends, see what interest is like. As for hints and tricks, I’ll let the more knowledgeable folks chime in. (edited)
messages.content_html: I’d ask around to see if anyone else locally is interested in playing with mesh comms. It seems like most of us are located in or near the bigger cities, and that’s where activity is starting. So unless you are setting up a network for your family and friends, see what interest is like. As for hints and tricks, I’ll let the more knowledgeable folks chime in. (edited)

messages.author: davidjay
messages.text: MeshMapper for CMH is Live! The Meshcore MeshMapper for the CMH region is now up and running. It's only seen adverts from my repeater and room, so it will zoom into Bexley when you go there. As the repeaters and rooms around Board RTLnet send adverts, they'll get added to the map. Anyone with connectivity to a repeater that can reach an observer in the CMH region can start wardriving. Active Observers Bexley Westerville What Next? What is wardriving? How does it work? Add your own observer. Get the App Android: Get It on the Playstore iOS: Get it on the App Store
messages.content_html:
MeshMapper for CMH is Live!

The Meshcore MeshMapper for the CMH region is now up and running. It's only seen adverts from my repeater and room, so it will zoom into Bexley when you go there. As the repeaters and rooms around Board RTLnet send adverts, they'll get added to the map. Anyone with connectivity to a repeater that can reach an observer in the CMH region can start wardriving.

Active Observers

  • Bexley
  • Westerville

What Next?

  • What is wardriving?
  • How does it work?

messages.author: Craig Wellert
messages.text: Hey guys! Im new to the mesh community. Im located in the wooster area. It seems to be a dead zone so far around me. Any advice to how I could further the mesh? I have made 3 pocket client-mutes running meshtastic for me and my buddies when we go camping, but I want to get in to meshcore and help grow that mesh as I see it being the future of community based meshes.

messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: Just saw this. You showed up in my contacts list. I’m just south of Downtown Dayton a couple of miles. As for Meshcore here in Dayton, it’s starting to kick off. There’s a sub category in the “DaytonMesh” Discord (alternative-mesh) that a bunch of us are gathering. You may want to join it to see whats up (DaytonMesh dot org is the web page). That Discord is mainly there for Meshtastic, but some folks are checking out MeshCore more. Also, one dude has gone way above and beyond to place some nodes around the south Dayton suburbs. Somehow there seems to be a link up between Springboro and Oxford too. (edited)

messages.author: FOMFOMWhiskeyTango78
messages.text: Hola MCOhio! I am working on getting a Meshcore Repeater stood up in Dublin/Powell, OH NWest Burbs of Columbus. Have everything physically ready but need to do some tweaks first.

Yarr
Hello from Toledo area! Flashed my Seeed P1 as a repeater and going to put it on the roof in the next few days. Anyone else here in NW OH?

messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: Just want to pass along that MeshCore is kinda blowing up here in the Dayton area. It’s still expanding, and folks are working on filling in gaps. If you are passing through Dayton or planning to attend Hamvention next month, feel free to check out the network!
messages.content_html: Just want to pass along that MeshCore is kinda blowing up here in the Dayton area. It’s still expanding, and folks are working on filling in gaps. If you are passing through Dayton or planning to attend Hamvention next month, feel free to check out the network!

messages.author: CamoCrabby
messages.text: If you are seeing this message on or past 4/23/26, I am moving over to the other MeshCore Discord. I have created another. “ Ohio” regional channel there. Feel free to give it a join, if you wish. Otherwise, catch you in down the line. 73!
messages.content_html: If you are seeing this message on or past 4/23/26, I am moving over to the other MeshCore Discord. I have created another. “ Ohio” regional channel there. Feel free to give it a join, if you wish. Otherwise, catch you in down the line. 73!

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
